page: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This low memory-to-vCPU ratio positions the instance within a compute-focused hardware class. It includes a bundled local storage capacity of 1890 GB and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in memory bandwidth and data compression. Uncached large-block memory read and write speeds rank in the top 25%, alongside multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and single-threaded gzip compression. Geekbench testing places its single-core and multi-core scores in the top 25%, with image processing performance reaching the top 10%. However, stress-ng CPU performance and Passmark database operations fall within the middle 50% average.

The instance exhibits a clear tradeoff between compute power and memory capacity. While it achieves top-tier performance in small-model LLM text generation, its large-model LLM text generation speed falls into the bottom 25% due to memory constraints. This profile makes the D32lds_v6 suitable for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as image processing, static web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference, rather than memory-heavy database systems or large-scale LLM deployments.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1890 GB local disk. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance is tailored for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive applications. Benchmark data highlights strong performance in uncached memory bandwidth, image processing, and small-model LLM text generation, all ranking in the top 25% or higher. Conversely, database operations and stress-ng CPU tests perform in the average middle tier, and large-model LLM inference is limited by the memory profile. The D32lds_v6 is best suited for workloads such as image processing, static web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, excelling in image processing and small-model LLM inference.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v6 is an Intel-based server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, bundled local storage, and high-throughput image and text processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
64.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Bundled 1890 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation
Strong uncached memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression speeds
Suited for static web serving, image processing, and small-scale inference
